name: structural_housing_quality
description: Measures the physical quality of the dwelling's basic construction materials — walls, roof, and floor — as indicators of structural adequacy and habitability. Together these items form a composite index of material precariousness.

name: basic_services_access
description: Captures the availability and adequacy of essential infrastructure services within the dwelling, including water supply frequency and delivery method, drainage, and electricity, reflecting the degree to which households are connected to public utility networks.

name: household_asset_endowment
description: Measures the stock of durable goods and domestic appliances present in the dwelling — from basic items like refrigerators and washing machines to modern assets like computers and solar panels — as a proxy for household socioeconomic status and living standards.

name: housing_tenure_security
description: Measures the legal and practical security of the household's occupancy arrangement, including ownership status, who holds title, how the dwelling was acquired, and whether it was obtained through a formal financing institution.

name: housing_problem_perception
description: Captures respondents' subjective evaluation of housing conditions in Mexico at a societal level, including their overall quality rating, perceived primary solutions, and attribution of institutional responsibility for resolving housing problems.
